evals.prompt: I need to build an agent that takes a research topic, searches for information, then writes a report based on findings
evals.expected_output: A Pipeline with two QuantedAgent steps, full production config: dotenv, env vars, skills/feedback folders, trace logging, soft budgets, restructurer, compact data models, no tool creation
assertions: Code imports from quanted_agents (not raw pydantic-ai or another framework)
Uses QuantedAgent class for agents, not raw pydantic_ai.Agent
Uses Pipeline to chain the researcher and writer steps sequentially
Model strings are read from environment variables or config, not hardcoded
Input and output types are Pydantic BaseModel subclasses
Main function is async and wrapped with asyncio.run()
Output type of researcher matches input type of writer (Pipeline type rule)
Each agent has skills_path configured from an environment variable
Each agent has feedback_path configured from an environment variable
Skill markdown files are created under agent_instructions/skills/<agent>/
Feedback folders exist with .gitkeep under agent_instructions/feedback/<agent>/
System prompts are minimal (core role only, not detailed instructions)
No tool functions are written — only TODO comments if tools would be beneficial
load_dotenv() is called before any os.environ.get() usage
Every agent has restructurer_model configured
Every agent has llm_call_limit and tool_call_limit with soft_limit=True
Every run() call passes traces_path
Inter-agent data models are compact (no massive dict/Any fields)

evals.prompt: Create a support ticket system where tickets get classified and routed to the right specialist agent
evals.expected_output: A Router with dispatcher and specialists, full production config: dotenv, env vars, skills/feedback folders, trace logging, soft budgets, restructurer, compact data models, no tool creation
assertions: Code imports from quanted_agents (not raw pydantic-ai or another framework)
Uses QuantedAgent class for agents, not raw pydantic_ai.Agent
Uses Router with a dispatcher and specialists dict
Dispatcher agent output_type is RoutingDecision
Model strings are read from environment variables or config, not hardcoded
Input and output types are Pydantic BaseModel subclasses
Main function is async and wrapped with asyncio.run()
At least 2 specialist agents are defined
Each agent has skills_path configured from an environment variable
Each agent has feedback_path configured from an environment variable
Skill markdown files are created under agent_instructions/skills/<agent>/
Feedback folders exist with .gitkeep under agent_instructions/feedback/<agent>/
System prompts are minimal (core role only, not detailed instructions)
No tool functions are written — only TODO comments if tools would be beneficial
load_dotenv() is called before any os.environ.get() usage
Every agent has restructurer_model configured
Every agent has llm_call_limit and tool_call_limit with soft_limit=True
Every run() call passes traces_path
Inter-agent data models are compact (no massive dict/Any fields)

evals.prompt: Build me a parent agent that orchestrates 3 child agents: a researcher, a writer, and a reviewer. The writer should use the researcher's findings.
evals.expected_output: Hierarchical agents with RunnableTool, ArtifactStore, WorkflowBudget(50/30), soft_limit=True, full production config: dotenv, env vars, skills/feedback folders, trace logging, compact data models, no tool creation
assertions: Code imports from quanted_agents (not raw pydantic-ai or another framework)
Uses QuantedAgent class for agents, not raw pydantic_ai.Agent
Uses RunnableTool to wrap child agents as tools for the parent
Uses ArtifactStore for sharing data between child agents
Writer's input_transform reads researcher's findings from the store
Model strings are read from environment variables or config, not hardcoded
Uses WorkflowBudget to limit resource usage across the hierarchy
Main function is async and wrapped with asyncio.run()
All 3 child agents (researcher, writer, reviewer) are defined
Each agent has skills_path configured from an environment variable
Each agent has feedback_path configured from an environment variable
Skill markdown files are created under agent_instructions/skills/<agent>/
Feedback folders exist with .gitkeep under agent_instructions/feedback/<agent>/
System prompts are minimal (core role only, not detailed instructions)
No tool functions are written — only TODO comments if tools would be beneficial
load_dotenv() is called before any os.environ.get() usage
Every agent has restructurer_model configured
Every agent has llm_call_limit and tool_call_limit with soft_limit=True
Every run() call passes traces_path
Inter-agent data models are compact (no massive dict/Any fields)
